[TRANSCRIPT — Project Nexus Kickoff Call]
[2024-03-04 10:02 AM | Attendees: Sarah Chen (PM), Dev Rao (Eng Lead), Priya Nair (Design), Marcus Webb (Stakeholder)]

Sarah: Okay I think everyone's on, uh, Dev can you hear us okay?

Dev: Yeah yeah I'm here, sorry I was muted.

Sarah: No worries. So, Marcus wanted to kick off the Nexus project today and—Marcus, do you want to set the stage?

Marcus: Sure. So the board has approved the budget and we need to ship the first version by end of Q2. That's non-negotiable, so everything else we decide today needs to work backwards from that.

Dev: End of Q2 meaning June 30th specifically or—

Marcus: June 30. Yes.

Dev: Okay. That's tight but we can make it work if we scope correctly.

Sarah: Right so the first thing I want to land is the tech stack decision. Dev, you had a recommendation?

Dev: Yeah so I've been going back and forth but I think we go with React on the frontend, FastAPI on the backend, Postgres for primary storage. That's my recommendation.

Priya: What about the mobile side, are we doing—I thought there was talk of a React Native wrapper?

Dev: I—honestly I'd deprioritize that for V1. Let's get a solid web app out first.

Marcus: Agreed, mobile can be V2.

Sarah: Okay so that's decided. React, FastAPI, Postgres. Dev owns the tech stack. [pause] Um, what about the authentication approach? I know there was a thing about whether we do our own auth or use an external provider.

Dev: External provider. Don't roll your own auth, ever, that's—that's just best practice. I'd recommend Auth0 or Firebase Auth.

Sarah: Do we have a preference between those two?

Dev: I'd lean Auth0 because we already have a contract with them through the enterprise plan. But honestly either works.

Marcus: Auth0 it is, if we already have the contract it's a no-brainer.

Sarah: Cool. Priya, design timeline—what are you thinking?

Priya: So I need the component library decision before I can finalize anything. Are we doing Material UI or building from scratch?

Dev: Let's do Material UI, I don't want the team spending time on custom components for V1.

Priya: Okay that works. If we're doing Material UI I can have wireframes ready in... two weeks? Maybe ten days if I push.

Sarah: Let's say two weeks. Action item for Priya: wireframes by March 18th.

Priya: Got it.

Sarah: And Dev—can you have the repo scaffolded and CI pipeline set up by end of this week?

Dev: Friday, yes.

Sarah: Great. Marcus, anything else from your side before we wrap up?

Marcus: Just—make sure the data stays in EU region. We have GDPR obligations and I don't want that to be an afterthought.

Dev: Yep, noted. We'll use AWS eu-west-1.

Sarah: Okay I'll send out a summary. So to recap: React/FastAPI/Postgres stack, Auth0 for authentication, Material UI component library, ship V1 by June 30, data in EU region. Dev scaffolds repo by Friday, Priya has wireframes by March 18. Anything I missed?

Dev: I think that covers it.

Marcus: Good call everyone.

[END OF TRANSCRIPT]
